steps.title: All buffers should be autoclaved or...
steps.instruction: All buffers should be autoclaved or filtered before use and kept at 4 °C.

steps.title: All dyes should be prepared fresh,...
steps.instruction: All dyes should be prepared fresh, immediately before use and be protected from light exposure as much as possible.

steps.instruction: Final buffer 20 mM Tris, pH 8.0 150 mM NaCl 10% glycerol

steps.title: Diazo dye Solid 4-nitrobenzenediazonium tetrafluoroborate 1×...

steps.title: Protein induction
steps.instruction: Protein induction

steps.title: Transform E
steps.instruction: Transform E. coli with recombinant Ac-AChBP with the N-terminal (His)6-tag in 200 mL of LB media with AMP (100 μg/mL) selection. Grow overnight at 37 °C with shaking.

steps.title: Inoculate the above into 1
steps.instruction: Inoculate the above into 1.3 L of LB media at 37 °C. Save 2 mL (out of 200 mL volume) to use as a blank for the spectrophotometer reading.

steps.title: Monitor cell growth by measuring the...
steps.instruction: Monitor cell growth by measuring the OD 600 approximately 2 h after inoculation.

steps.title: When the absorbance reaches 0
steps.instruction: When the absorbance reaches 0.2, add IPTG to a final concentration of 1mM to induce protein expression. Incubate at 16 °C overnight. A

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6,000× g for 15 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6,000× g for 15 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant, resuspend the pellet...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant, resuspend the pellet in fresh LB media, and repeat the centrifugation process.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and keep the cell pellet. Optional
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and keep the cell pellet. Optional: This pellet can be frozen at -80 °C for up to four months. A

steps.title: Lysis and protein purification
steps.instruction: Lysis and protein purification

steps.title: Freshly prepare the lysis, resuspension, elution,...
steps.instruction: Freshly prepare the lysis, resuspension, elution, and final buffers (see Recipes).

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 50 mL of lysis buffer with 500 μL of 10 mg/mL DNase, 50 mg of lysozyme, and one Complete EDTA-free mini protease inhibitor tablet.

steps.title: Sonicate the cells and then centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the cells and then centrifuge at 39,000× g for 45 min. Collect the supernatant (lysis fraction).

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 50 mL of resuspension buffer (Buffer A) with one Complete EDTA-free mini protease inhibitor tablet.

steps.title: Sonicate the cells and then centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the cells and then centrifuge at 39,000× g for 30 min. Collect the supernatant (load fraction) and the pellet (pellet fraction).

steps.title: Equilibrate the Ni-NTA column with Buffer A
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the Ni-NTA column with Buffer A.

steps.title: Wash the column with 30–50 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash the column with 30–50 mL of Buffer A (supernatant from step A3.

steps.title: to remove nonspecific binding proteins
steps.instruction: to remove nonspecific binding proteins. Save the flowthrough (wash fraction).

steps.title: Elute the protein with 15–20 mL...
steps.instruction: Elute the protein with 15–20 mL of Buffer B (elution fraction). A

steps.title: Confirmation of protein expression
steps.instruction: Confirmation of protein expression

steps.title: Perform a Bradford assay to determine...
steps.instruction: Perform a Bradford assay to determine protein concentration for each fraction (pellet, lysis, load, wash, and elution).

steps.title: Load equal amounts of protein from...
steps.instruction: Load equal amounts of protein from each fraction onto a single SDS-PAGE gel and run the gel, ensuring complete separation of the molecular weight marker bands between 60 and 10 kDa.

steps.title: Stain the gel using a Coomassie...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel using a Coomassie solution stain (see manufacturer’s instructions).

steps.title: Confirm the expression of the Ac-AChBP...
steps.instruction: Confirm the expression of the Ac-AChBP subunit as the major protein product on the gel. The Ac-AChBP subunit is expected to run at ~28 kDa [6]. A

steps.title: Protein concentration and buffer exchange
steps.instruction: Protein concentration and buffer exchange

steps.title: Concentrate the eluted protein using a...
steps.instruction: Concentrate the eluted protein using a 30 kDa Amicon Ultra Centrifugal filter for 15 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the flowthrough and refill the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flowthrough and refill the filter with the final buffer to remove imidazole.

steps.title: Repeat the concentration process two times...
steps.instruction: Repeat the concentration process two times to complete the buffer exchange. B. Protein paint B

steps.title: Preparing the Ac-AChBP and ligand
steps.instruction: Preparing the Ac-AChBP and ligand

steps.title: Determine the appropriate number of samples...
steps.instruction: Determine the appropriate number of samples and label a sterile Eppendorf protein Lo-Bind 1.5 mL tube for each. For example, use one tube for every individual Ac-AChBP unpainted condition, one tube for every Ac-AChBP painted condition, and one tube for every Ac-AChBP complex [Ac-AChBP and the tested ligand (α-Bungarotoxin)] painted condition. Note: Additional ligands, including choline, nicotine, and amyloid β-Protein (1-42), can also be tested.

steps.title: Prepare protein complexes in solution at...
steps.instruction: Prepare protein complexes in solution at 1:10 molar ratio of Ac-AChBP:ligand. For a 65 μL total solution, for example, prepare: 2.94 μL of Ac-AChBP 1.60 μL of α-Bungarotoxin 61.26 μL of 1× PBS For control samples that do not contain a ligand, bring the final volume up to 65 μL with 1× PBS.

steps.title: Place the protein paint complex on...
steps.instruction: Place the protein paint complex on a rotator and incubate at room temperature for 1 h with gentle mixing at 20 rpm. Note: Ensure that the solution is mixed well during the incubation.

steps.title: Prepare the appropriate number of Sephadex columns as samples
steps.instruction: Prepare the appropriate number of Sephadex columns as samples. Give each column a sharp downward shake to ensure that the resin moves to the bottom of the column. Remove the cap and place the column in a 2 mL Eppendorf collection tube.

steps.title: Using a 20–200 μL pipette, remove...
steps.instruction: Using a 20–200 μL pipette, remove any remaining Sephadex gel from inside of the cap and place it in the column.

steps.title: Spin the Sephadex columns at 1,000× g for 1 min
steps.instruction: Spin the Sephadex columns at 1,000× g for 1 min. Note: Make sure there are no air pockets within the column as this might interfere with dye filtration.

steps.title: Add 400 μL of MilliQ water...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μL of MilliQ water and let sit at room temperature until the protein paint complex incubation step (step B1.

steps.title: Protein paint experiment
steps.instruction: Protein paint experiment

steps.title: Add 5 μL of diazo dye...
steps.instruction: Add 5 μL of diazo dye (5 mg/mL, 1 mg in 200 μL of PBS, prepared fresh) to each painted protein sample. Add 5 μL of 1× PBS to each sample that will be unpainted.

steps.title: Incubate samples for 30 min at...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 30 min at room temperature on a rotator.

steps.title: Spin all columns at 1,000× g for 1 min
steps.instruction: Spin all columns at 1,000× g for 1 min. Add 5 μL of 5 mg/mL NHS ester dye to the painted protein samples. Add 5 μL of DMSO to the unpainted sample. Note: The volume must be consistent across the painted and unpainted groups during the entire experiment.

steps.title: Place all samples on a tube...
steps.instruction: Place all samples on a tube rotator and incubate for 30 min at room temperature.

steps.title: During the incubation period, add 400...
steps.instruction: During the incubation period, add 400 μL of MilliQ water to each Sephadex column and centrifuge at 1,000× g for 2 min.

steps.title: Remove the Sephadex columns from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the Sephadex columns from the 2 mL tubes and place in new labeled Eppendorf Lo-Bind 1.5 mL tubes. Ensure there are no air pockets or cracks in the resin before adding samples. If these are present, repeat the previous step by adding MilliQ water and spinning the columns once again at the same settings.

steps.title: When the incubation is complete, transfer...
steps.instruction: When the incubation is complete, transfer the entire 75 μL of sample from each tube to the appropriate Sephadex column. Note: Carefully add the sample in the middle and avoid touching the resin with your pipette tip.

steps.title: Centrifuge all samples at 1,000× g for 1 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all samples at 1,000× g for 1 min. Throw away columns and retain the flowthrough. B

steps.title: Denature proteins and add pre-digestion control
steps.instruction: Denature proteins and add pre-digestion control

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.8 μL of 250 μg/mL lysozyme stock to each sample.

steps.title: Add 25 μL of 8 M...
steps.instruction: Add 25 μL of 8 M urea to each sample, for a final volume of 100 μL and a final urea concentration of 2M.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.1 μL of 1 M DTT to each sample, for a final volume of 101.1 μL and a final DTT concentration of 10mM.

steps.title: Incubate samples at 37 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at 37 °C for 30 min using a water bath.

steps.title: Add 12 μL of iodoacetamide to...
steps.instruction: Add 12 μL of iodoacetamide to each sample, for a final volume of 113.1 μL and a final iodoacetamide concentration of 50 mM.

steps.title: Incubate samples at room temperature in...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at room temperature in a dark place for at least 15 min. B

steps.title: Trypsin digestion of protein complex
steps.instruction: Trypsin digestion of protein complex

steps.title: Add the following reagents according to the recipe below
steps.instruction: Add the following reagents according to the recipe below: 86.32 μL of MilliQ H 2 O (ultrapure water) 22 μL of ammonium bicarbonate 0.58 μL of trypsin* *If your calculations for a 1:10 trypsin ratio result in a trypsin volume less than 0.5 μL, just add 0.5 μL . This brings the final sample volume to 222 μL, final urea concentration to under 1M, ammonium bicarbonate to 50mM, and trypsin to 1:10 ratio.

steps.title: Allow samples to digest overnight in...
steps.instruction: Allow samples to digest overnight in a hot water bath at 37 °C.

steps.title: Halt digestion by adding 5 μL...
steps.instruction: Halt digestion by adding 5 μL of acetic acid, ~2% acetic acid in the final sample (or you can skip straight to preparing the C-18 columns).

steps.title: Store samples at -20 °C until...
steps.instruction: Store samples at -20 °C until they can be prepped for mass spectrometry. B

steps.title: Prepare C-18 columns
steps.instruction: Prepare C-18 columns

steps.title: Thaw samples and add 75 μL...
steps.instruction: Thaw samples and add 75 μL of sample buffer to each sample.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 1,000× g for 1 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 1,000× g for 1 min. For technical replicates, divide each sample into two 150 μL aliquots in protein Lo-Bind 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. Alternatively, pass all 150 μL of sample through the column at one time.

steps.title: Follow the C-18 spin column manufacturer’s...
steps.instruction: Follow the C-18 spin column manufacturer’s directions to remove salt from samples.

steps.title: For the final spin column step,...
steps.instruction: For the final spin column step, elute each sample into a clean 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Dry samples under nitrogen at 40...
steps.instruction: Dry samples under nitrogen at 40 °C for roughly 10 min. Monitor closely to ensure no sample is blown out of the tube. Place dried peptides at -20 °C until mass spectrometry analysis can be performed.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated across various experiments in the following research articles: Graur et al. [6]. Protein Painting Mass Spectrometry in the Discovery of Interaction Sites within the Acetylcholine Binding Protein. ACS Chemical Neuroscience . Haymond et al. [12]. Protein painting, an optimized MS-based technique, reveals functionally relevant interfaces of the PD-1/PD-L1 complex and the YAP2/ZO-1 complex. The Journal of Biological Chemistry . Luchini et al. [8]. Protein painting reveals solvent-excluded drug targets hidden within native protein-protein interfaces. Nature Communications .
